Malaria exists in 103 countries worldwide, affecting 3.3. billion people, but about 90% of malaria-related deaths occur in sub-Saharan Africa. The majority of those affected are children under the age of five...if you don't know 😎
Used $83 to treat Malaria parasite and am still having this banging headache 😔

Malaria is spread by parasites. Five different parasites can cause malaria in humans, but the Plasmodium falciparum parasite is the most deadly. The parasites enter the human bloodstream through the bite of an infected mosquito...if you don't know 😎

NEVER TAKE MALARIA TREATMENT with👇
Fruits/Juice/Vitamin C
Multivitamins
Blood tonic/capsules
Folic acid (if pregnant, take low dose)
Iron Supplements.

ALSO NEVER TAKE ANTIBIOTICS with
Grape Juice, Dairy foods (milk), Caffeine, Alcohol, Antacids, Magnesium/Calcium supplements

SOME COMPLICATIONS OF MALARIA
👉Anaemia: shortage of blood
👉kidney failure
👉Cerebral malaria, 
👉liver failure 
👉Shock – a sudden drop in blood pressure
👉Pulmonary oedema – a build-up of fluid in the lungs
👉Acute respiratory distress  syndrome (ARDS)
👉Low blood sugar

🇳🇬ESTIMATED MALARIA CASES & DEATHS, 2021

CASES
2017 — 57.9m
2018 — 59.7m
2019 — 61.4m
2020 — 65.1m
2021 — 64.4m

DEATHS
2017 — 177K
2018 — 187K
2019 — 187K
2020 — 196K
2021 — 194K

(World Malaria Report 2022)
(StatiSense)

GLOBAL MALARIA - TOP 5 NATIONS, 2021

CASES
1 🇳🇬Nigeria — 26.6%
2 🇨🇩Congo Dr — 12.3%
3 🇺🇬Uganda — 5.1%
4 🇲🇿Mozambique — 4.1%
5 🇦🇴Angola — 3.4%

DEATHS
1 🇳🇬Nigeria — 31.3%
2 🇨🇩Congo DR — 12.6%
3 🇹🇿Tanzania — 4.1%
4 🇳🇪Niger — 3.9%
5 🇲🇿Mozambique — 3.8%

(WMR 2022)
(StatiSense)

GLOBAL MALARIA CASES 2021

Nigeria, Congo DR, Uganda, and Mozambique accounted for almost half of all malaria cases globally.

In rare cases, people have contracted malaria through a blood transfusion.

DID YOU KNOW? In Africa alone, one child dies every minute from malaria
Children and pregnant women are most at risk of serious illness if they contract malaria.
In Africa alone, one child dies every minute from malaria.
Untreated malaria can cause severe anemia and can quickly become life threatening. Seek medical treatment immediately, before symptoms get worse, especially in children and pregnant mothers.

If you live in a malaria area or you have been in one, do not ignore the following symptoms of malaria . . .
High temperature (fever)
Sweats
Shaking chills
Headache
Muscle aches
Fatigue
Nausea
Vomiting
Diarrhea

Also, it would be wise to speak to your physician about things you need to be aware of with regard to your personal health history.

During your visit, follow the guidelines that are presented in this article for those who live in a land where malaria is endemic.

If you are planning to visit a land where malaria is endemic . . .
Get current information before you travel. The type of malaria parasite common in one region may differ from that in another, and this affects which type of medicine is most effective.

A human can get the malaria parasite from an infected mosquito. Conversely, a noninfected mosquito can get the malaria parasite from biting an infected human. Then the infected mosquito can pass the parasite to another human

3 HOW CAN YOU PROTECT YOURSELF?
If you live in a land where malaria is endemic . . .
Use a bed net or a mosquito net. It should be
treated with insecticide.
free of any holes or tears.
tucked completely under the mattress.
Use indoor residual spraying in your home.
The cycle of red blood cell invasion and rupture continues. The infected person typically manifests symptoms of malaria each time the red blood cells rupture.

2 HOW DOES MALARIA SPREAD?
Malaria parasites​—protozoans called Plasmodia—​are introduced into the human bloodstream through the bite of a female Anopheles mosquito.
The parasites find their way into the infected person’s liver cells, where the parasites multiply.
